info.name: Employee GraphQL API
info.description:
Complete API testing collection for the Laravel Employee API.

Endpoints:
- GraphQL: POST {{baseUrl}}/api/graphql (login, me, employees, employee, updateEmployee, deleteEmployee, importEmployees, importStatus)
- REST: GET {{baseUrl}}/api/employees/export (Passport-guarded binary download)

Quick start:
1. Run **Auth > Login** first — it stores the access token in the `token` collection variable, and every other request sends it as a Bearer token automatically.
2. Run the other requests in any order.

Notes:
- Default credentials: admin / password.
- The import is asynchronous: it returns an `import_id` (stored in the `importId` variable). Poll **Import & Export > Import Status** to watch progress. The backend `php artisan queue:work` must be running for the import to be applied.
- For **Export**, use the "Send and Download" button (it returns a binary .xlsx).

exec: const token = json && json.data && json.data.login && json.data.login.access_token;

request.description: Exchange username/password for a Passport access token. The test script stores `access_token` into the `token` collection variable used by every other request.
Returns the authenticated user. Returns null when the token is missing/invalid.
Paginated employee list. `first` max is 100. Change `page` to walk pages.
Fetch a single employee by id (uses the `employeeId` collection variable).
Partial update — send only the fields you want to change (besides `id`). A duplicate email returns a validation error under `extensions.validation` keyed `input.email`.
Delete an employee by id and return the deleted record.

exec: const id = json && json.data && json.data.importEmployees && json.data.importEmployees.import_id;
console.log('Saved importId — run "Import Status" to track progress.');

request.description:
Multipart upload following the graphql-multipart-request-spec.

Before sending, click the `0` form field and choose a spreadsheet file. Do NOT set a Content-Type header — Postman adds the multipart boundary automatically.

The import runs asynchronously on the queue worker and returns an `import_id` (saved to the `importId` variable by the test script).
Poll live import progress by `import_id`. `status` goes queued -> processing -> completed; `processed`/`total` drive a progress bar.
Passport-guarded REST endpoint that streams all employees as an .xlsx. Use the "Send and Download" button to save the file. Without a valid Bearer token it returns HTTP 401.
